events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need respond with raw JSON only, matching schema. Need analyze scenario. Field service, electrical terminations & splices, rough-complete. Condition: insufficient torque (thermal signature - IR modality). Work under review. Need identify component or condition and visible defect cues. Since insufficient torque causes high resistance connection, thermal anomaly under IR, NEC 110.14 requires terminals to be torqued per manufacturer specs. So decision fail due to non-compliant/dangerous (thermal hazard). Risk high or critical? Insufficient torque can cause overheating, fire. In service call, operating asset, maybe high. Work_stage rough-complete. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80? value maybe temperature not provided, leave 0. findings: loose connection, inadequate torque, abnormal thermal signature. actions: de-energize, torque terminals per manufacturer/OEM spec, re-inspect IR, verify with calibrated torque screwdriver. rationale. references NEC 110.14. Need no prose. Ensure valid JSON with all fields.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Termination/splice torque is below required value",
    "Infrared inspection shows abnormal thermal signature at the connection",
    "High-resistance connection risk due to insufficient contact pressure",
    "Potential for overheating, conductor/insulation damage, or fire"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize and verify zero energy state",
    "Clean and inspect terminal/splice contact surfaces",
    "Re-torque all terminations and splices to OEM/manufacturer specifications using a calibrated torque tool",
    "Re-perform infrared thermography under load to confirm acceptable thermal performance",
    "Document torque values and re-inspection results"
  ],
  "rationale": "Insufficient torque on electrical terminations and splices creates a high-resistance connection that produces a detectable thermal signature. NEC 110.14 requires terminations to be installed and torqued per manufacturer specifications; an overheating connection in an installed, operating asset is a fire and equipment-damage hazard, so the condition fails inspection.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.14"]
}
